content:
3 min read
Just now

--

What the shift from 'talk to AI' to 'AI does things for you' actually means and how you can build it from Kampala.
Let me tell you about the last three months of
my life.
I am a CS student in Uganda. I have been coding since secondary school. And lately, I
have been watching AI shift from something you chat with into something that actually
does work opens files, writes code, searches the web, sends emails, makes decisions.
inside a workflow all on its own.
This is what people mean when they say 'agentic AI.' And it is not science fiction. It is available now, it is free to experiment with, and if you understand it early, you are ahead of most developers in the world not just in Uganda.

graphic showing the difference between a chatbot (user asks, AIresponds) and an agent (AI plans, tools, acts, loops).
So What Exactly Is an AI Agent?
A regular chatbot responds. An AI agent acts.
Technically, an agent is an LLM (large language model) with access to tools things like
a web search function, a code execution environment, a file system, or an API. The model
reads the goal, decides which tools to use, uses them, reads the results, and keeps going
until the task is done.

Example: 'Find me the three most cited computer science papers published in East Africa
in 2024, summarise them, and put them in a Google Doc.' That is a task that requires
search, reading, summarising, and writing. An agent can do it.

https://python.langchain.com/docs/modules/agents
The Market Numbers (Why Everyone Is Paying Attention)
The agentic AI market was worth $8.6 billion in 2025 and is projected to hit $263 billion by
2035 a 40% annual growth rate. To put that in perspective: that is roughly the size of the
entire Ugandan economy doubling every two and a half years.
But money is the secondary story.

The primary story is that agents are going to automate
significant chunks of digital work coding, research, customer service, data analysis.

Developers who understand how to build and control these systems will be extremely
valuable.

Bar chart: Agentic AI market growth (2025) to (2035).How You Can Build an Agent Right Now For Free
Here is the simplest agent you can build this weekend in Python:
Step 1: Install LangChain and an LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or use a free Groq
API key for LLaMA 3).
Step 2: Give the model a tool start with just web search or a Python REPL.
Step 3: Give it a goal, not a question. 'Research and summarise X' not 'What is X?'
Step 4: Add memory so it can reference earlier steps in the same task.

https://console.groq.com
https://python.langchain.com
The African Opportunity Nobody Is Talking About
Most agentic AI applications being built today are for Western enterprise workflows: CRM systems, Salesforce integrations, corporate email. That is a crowded market.
But agents that handle mobile money reconciliation, NSSF form submissions, KCCA permit
applications, or M-Pesa business workflows? Those are not being built. That gap is the opportunity.
If you are in Uganda and you understand agentic AI, the most valuable thing you can do is not compete with Silicon Valley on their turf. Build for problems that
Silicon Valley does not even know exist.

By a Ugandan CS Student.
